title: CLM 2.0 — Club Mahindra (Mahindra Holidays)
subtitle: Transforming lifecycle marketing into a governed, data-driven system
client: Club Mahindra / Mahindra Holidays & Resorts India Ltd.
overview: Club Mahindra set out to scale its Customer Lifecycle Marketing as memberships, resorts, and lines of business grew in complexity. Strong transactional and engagement data existed across EDW, CleverTap, SFMC, and booking systems — but CLM execution faced challenges due to fragmented governance, static segmentation, and delayed performance insights.

Phase II focused on moving from execution-heavy CRM to a governed, data-led lifecycle marketing model. Attributics was engaged to redesign the CLM operating framework — establishing governance controls, rebuilding segmentation strategy, and creating unified visibility across campaigns and channels.

challenge.subtitle: Fragmented Governance
challenge.description: With multiple lines of business (LOBs), channels, and campaign teams operating independently, there was no centralized framework to manage messaging frequency, lifecycle priority, or channel conflicts. This led to over-messaging, inconsistent member experiences, and campaign overlaps.

solution.subtitle: Lifecycle Governance & Control
solution.description: Establish centralized governance for messaging frequency, lifecycle priority, suppressions, and channel conflicts across all LOBs.

process.title: Governance Framework
process.description: Established centralized lifecycle definitions, messaging frequency caps, channel prioritization, and suppression logic. Implemented a unified campaign calendar to eliminate overlaps and ensure consistent communication across lines of business.

conclusion: Through a structured, data-led approach, Attributics helped Club Mahindra evolve its CLM from campaign execution to a governed, performance-driven operating model. By embedding a centralised governance framework, rebuilding segmentation strategy, and unifying performance visibility, Club Mahindra gained consistent control over its member communications — reducing over-messaging, improving campaign relevance, and creating a scalable foundation for sustained lifecycle marketing growth.
